entry.title: Re: ENQUEUE_E_CRM_ORDER
entry.content: @raymond_giuseppi If your program is terminated, the LUW is also terminated, so you cannot maintain the lock in this case. What is the solution to this?

entry.title: Re: How to Develop an OData Service: CURD operations, Pag...
entry.content:
hi,

why is this posted under Q&A? It looks much more like a blog post.. 

Addiontal comment: The code could use some "clean"-up. Have a look at https://github.com/SAP/styleguides/blob/main/clean-abap/CleanABAP.md to get an idea. 

Furthermore, there are already several blogs on the topic, you might want to research and build up on the existing knowledge of the community by posting what's new on your approach or what the reader can learn he or she didn't learn by reading the other blogs.

Best Regards
Hendrik

entry.title: How to customize the column list in the table settings? (fiori elements)
entry.content:
I have a fiori elements app where I created a list report. This has a table with columns. In the settings of the table I can choose which additional columns I want to be displayed or which columns i want to hide. How can i customize this column list. 

 

My goal is to delete some columns of that column list in the settings, because the user should not be able to choose them.

entry.title: PMGM Calibration Session Audit report
entry.content: We are unable to generate a valid system audit report based on user changes, based on changes to employee scores in PMGM

entry.title: Re: SAP Focused Run 4.0 FP02, unconfigured versus SAP Foc...
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

The first appliance template - SAP Focused Run 4.0 FP02, unconfigured - is a standard installation version with no configurations on top. With regards to the second one - SAP Focused Run 4.0 FP02 (configured) - the SAP Focused Run system is configured according to the respective Master Guide (https://help.sap.com/viewer/product/FRUN/latest/en-US?task=implement_task) as a managed system, therefore several functionalities can be explored without any additional configuration.

For more information, see the both appliance template getting started guides (https://caldocs.hana.ondemand.com/caldocs/help/130453cf-8bea-41dc-a692-7d6052e10e2d_Getting_Started_Guide_v5.pdf , https://caldocs.hana.ondemand.com/caldocs/help/0686c57a-79d8-40c7-a45d-1677e64d79db_Getting_Started_Guide_v2.pdf).

Best regards,

Tsvetinka

entry.title: how to know the requester Department for material reservation in SAP MM
entry.content: How to know the requester Department for material reservation in SAP MM if there materials required for issuing not for cost center ?

entry.title: Support Spotlight: Best Practices when Re-installing in SAP Business One
entry.content:
In this blog post our expert Paul Shannon from the System team in SAP Business One Support, shares a few handy tips on how and why a re-install can be done and why in some cases it might be a good idea.

Many different types of software can face performance issues or data corruption over time. Depending on the specific issue, it can often be more efficient to perform a clean install than spend time attempting to diagnose these problems. This also applies to SAP Business One. Another advantage of a clean SAP Business One install is removing or cleaning up any remnants of possible previous installations which can solve multiple issues.

The blog covers re-installing the SAP Business One Client, we will look at the services, both in MSSQL and HANA environment and finally some documentation tips to help you along.

SAP Business One Client

The first component we will look at is the SAP Business One Client.

There are a couple of ways a corrupted SAP Business One Client may show symptoms:
- A user is working in the client when it shuts down unexpectedly
- Attempting to install the SAP Business One Client fails with an error.

If the client is unstable or shuts down unexpectedly, it may simply be an issue with the SAP Business One user rather than the actual client. The SAP Business One user preferences may be corrupted in some way. This can be resolved by copying preferences from another user and applying them to the corrupted user. This procedure is covered in the below link:
Copying User Preferences to Another User or User Group | SAP Help Portal

However, if the issue lies with the client itself, you can follow the below steps to rectify this:

1. Uninstall each add-on solution from your SAP Business One client.
2. Uninstall DI API using Add/Remove Programs in Control Panel.
3. Uninstall SAP Business One client using Add/Remove Programs in Control Panel.
4. Delete the directory %temp%/SM_OBS_DLL.
5. Reinstall client-side components in the following order:

a. SAP Business One client
b. Add-on solutions, if applicable

Note: DI API is automatically installed during the SAP Business One client installation.

In the event the client installation fails for some reason, we have further options. Firstly, we should ensure we have cleaned the remnants of the previous installation correctly and that the installation package we are using is not corrupt itself:

  • Remove the "SAP" folder from the path - "C:\Users\<user_name>\AppData\Local\"
  • Download the SAP Business One installation or upgrade package directly from the SAP Launchpad to ensure that the original package was not corrupted.
  • Copy the installation package to the machine desktop and run the installation from there instead of installing the package from the shared folder. This step can help to avoid any network or permission issues caused by a remote installation.

The next option we have is to clean any SAP Business One Client components in the Windows registry. Delete SAP registry keys under HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE and HKEY_CURRENT_USER.

Important: Please keep in mind to perform a registry backup before any modification. Any registry modifications are done at your own risk.

For more information, please see
https://support.microsoft.com/en-in/help/322756/how-to-back-up-and-restore-the-registry-in-windows.

 

SAP Business One Services

Depending on whether you are running a HANA or MSSQL version of SAP Business One, we have different procedures for a clean installation of the services. However, in principle these have the same steps:

  • Remove existing components completely, manually force the uninstall if necessary
  • Delete or rename existing installation folders
  • Attempt a fresh install of all components

Important: Before attempting any of the below steps, ensure you have backed up your whole server if possible (HANA), SLDModel.SLDDatadatabase and Windows registry (MSSQL).

 

For an SAP Business One MSSQL environment we can first attempt an uninstall via Add/Remove programs (Control Panel → search for SAP Business One ServerTools → Uninstall).

If this does not work or for some reason you find that ServerTools is not listed in Control Panel, we have the option to manually force the uninstallation of the component by running cmd.exe as Administrator using the below command:

msiexec.exe /x {"ProductCode"}

You can find the correct product code of the component in the setup.ini file in the upgrade package (Packages → ServerTools → setup.ini file). Then backup SLDModel.SLDDatadatabase via MSSQL Server Management Studio before deleting it. From this point we can delete or rename the old installation folder.

Next, to be sure we have cleared out any remnants of the previous install completely, you can clean the registry. Again, be sure to back up the registry before this next step. Open regedit.exe and then search for "ProductCode" (Packages → Server Tools → setup.ini file). After these steps and before attempting the new install, reboot your machine.

In SAP Business One version 10 FP2111 and later, we have the option of the new “Components Wizard”. This new feature can greatly simplify this process:

  • Go to Add/Remove programs → Control Panel → search for SAP Business One Components Wizard → Uninstall.
  • Delete or rename the folder SAP Business One Servertools from "C:\Program Files\SAP".
  • To unregister or perform a clean uninstall the SAP Business One Servertools, select each component under \\..\\B1100_PL2111\Packages.x64\ComponentsWizard\MSI, right click and select Uninstall.
  • Reboot the machine.

It should now be possible to complete a fresh install using the Server Tools/Components Wizard installer.

 

For an SAP Business One HANA Environment it is possible to perform a forced uninstallation using the PuTTY command window. First back up the whole server if possible and back up the SAP HANA instance.

Execute the following command as root to list all SAP Business One related rpm packages:
rpm -qa | grep B1

If all the packages listed from the above step are SAP Business One related, use the following command to uninstall them all:
rpm -qa | grep B1 | xargs rpm -ev

Otherwise, it is possible to uninstall them one by one with the command:
rpm -ev

Next, we will need to open SAP HANA Studio. For SAP Business One version 10.0, all database administration tasks must be performed at tenant level (usually NDB@NDB) instead of System ID (usually NDB) level.

First, drop the schemas SBOCOMMON and SLDDATA by right-clicking and selecting "Delete". For SAP Business One version 10.0 FP2208 and later, also drop schema B1AS.

Drop the user COMMON by going to "Security"->"Users"->COMMON. Right-click and select "Delete". For SAP Business One version 10.0, also drop the below users:
B1_xxxxxx_RW,
B1_xxxxxx_RO,
SAPB1_DB_USER_xxxxxx,
B1_SBOCOMMON,
B1_SBOCOMMON_RW users with Restricted option.

Note: Do not drop the B1_xxxxxx_RW, B1_xxxxxx_RO users with CASCADE in case of any dependencies.

Delete or rename the folder /usr/sap/SAPBusinessOne and then reboot the server to release any ports occupied by deleted processes. Lastly, remove the SAP service control file, using the command:
rm -rf /etc/init.d/sapb1servertools.

We are now ready to launch the installation wizard to install server components.

To do that using PuTTY, go to the directory /Packages.linux/ServerComponents/ and execute the command:
./install

Documentation

For more detailed information on the above topics, check the SAP Notes in the below links:
2432304 - Troubleshooting Client Installation Issues
1989534 - Troubleshooting Guide for Corrupted SAP Business One Client Installation or Configuration
2706617 - UI Configuration Templates_Function, Definition, Errors and FAQ
2788059 - How to Perform Clean Reinstall of SAP Business One, version for MSSQL Server Tools
2538555 - How to Perform Clean Reinstall of SAP Business One, version for SAP HANA Server Components

Support Spotlight Series
We hope this read has been informative and beneficial. For more blog posts and tips from the SAP Business One Support team, please visit our space on the communities:
https://blogs.sap.com/tag/b1-support-spotlight/

For questions about SAP Business One, please submit your question here.

entry.title: Re: Connecting SAP Data Warehouse Cloud OData API with PowerBI via a Blank Query
entry.content:
Hi @ENES_SASMAZ,

This 404 issue with metadata requests to catalog has been identified in SAP Datasphere 2024.11 release. A fix has already been created and will be available in the next SAP Datasphere release.

Sorry about the inconvenience

Best Regards
Gustavo Kath

entry.title: Dump on HR_EVENT_UPDATE - DBSQL Duplicate key
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I am trying to update/insert time record in the database TEVEN  to populate in PA61, through function modules BAPI_CC1_UPLOAD_TIMEEVENT, HR_CC1_TIMEEVENT_POST, but  I am getting the ERROR : DBSQL_DUPLICATE_KEY_ERROR in the HR_EVENT_UPDATE  which is inside of HR_CC1_TIMEEVENT_POST, which is preventing time records to be saved.   

I kindly ask you to give insights on how to resolve this .

Thank you in advance

entry.title: Click to expand subreport
entry.content:
I know this should work - and annoyed with myself for not figuring it out.

Main report with two subreports - one residing in Da section and the other in Db.  Each subreport has Header, Details, and Footer section.  Header and Details are hidden, so that only the footers appear in the main report.

I want to be able to click on a subreport in the main report view and have the full subreport open, with headers, details, and footer visible.

Why can't I make this work?

Please let me know the details steps which need to be followed to accomplish this requirement, and also which interface to be used to do the transaction.

 

Thank you in Advance

Regards

Hithesh

entry.title: Re: standard LIKP table not getting activated after append structure and doing se14
entry.content: You can't know the time needed to adjust, you'll see. Next time, adjust in background. TYPELOAD_NEW_VERSION is normal if at the same time you adjust and you run a program using anything related to your adjusted table. Search the Web for more information.

entry.title: Re: Transit Procedure Questions

entry.title: Introducing Identity and Authentication Management in SAP Business One Cloud
entry.content:
Identity and Authentication Management in SAP Business One Cloud

If you’re working on SAP Business One in a Cloud environment, there is a good chance you are using Cloud Control Center (CCC) to manage the product's Lifecycle operations leveraging from its integration with Microsoft’s Active Directory service. Since CCC’s launch, the built-in integration to Active Directory service has served as the primary backbone for managing users access to SAP Business One in a secured and reliable manner.
With CCC’s PL 20, we’re taking it one step further by adding the option to use Identity Provider (IDP) services for signing-in to SAP Business One. By utilizing IDP services in CCC, hosting providers can offer their customers a seamless user experience by signing in to SAP Business One with their own IDP accounts (e.g. same Azure account they may use for MS365) and utilizing security best practices such as Two Factor Authentication.

Getting started

If you are familiar with Identity and Authentication management (IAM) solution in SAP Business One (Click here to read my latest blog on it), good news 😎 you are well on the way to mastering the setup of IAM also in Cloud Control Center as the configuration steps are almost identical.
To configure IAM in Cloud Control Center, follow the five simple steps below:

1️⃣  Register FP 2405 as a software repository in CCC

2️⃣  Activate “Enable Third Party Identity Provider” Global Setting

3️⃣  Add an Identity Provider and activate it

4️⃣  Add an Identity Provider user and bind it to company users

5️⃣  Sign-in to SAP Business One with an IDP user


Here’s a quick 📹 (no audio) capturing the steps above.


Take aways

Now that you got a hang of it, here are a few tips and tricks to take with you:

  • “Enable Third Party Identity Provider” Global Setting
    The new “Enable Third Party Identity Provider” Global Setting will be automatically visible in Cloud Control Center PL 20 once version SAP Business One 10.0 FP 2405 (or higher) is registered as a software repository.  
    The option to Sign-in to SAP Business One with an Identity Provider (via Open-ID Connect) is only available for Service units on version SAP Business One 10.0 FP 2405 or higher.

  • “Enable Mapping of IDP User to Active Directory User” checkbox
    A major advantage of CCC’s built-in integration with Active directory is its ability to automatically manage folder permissions and presentation server access for Active Directory users based on their Service Unit allocation in Cloud Control Center.
    To continue leveraging from this feature, make sure you tick the “Enable Mapping of IDP User to Active Directory User” checkbox during IDP user creation.
    With this mapping, a customer user can log into Remote Desktop Services with the Windows domain user account, and then Sign-in to SAP Business One client with the IDP user account.

  • Single Sign-On
    When working with the built-in Domain Active Directory as the identity provider, Active Directory users will continue leveraging from a Single Sign-On (SSO) experience as existed in previous CCC versions, under a new “Choose Company” window.

  • Partner Support User (PSU)
    To continue using the PSU functionality with IDP users, the Operator’s IDP user must have a mapping to an Active Directory User.

  • Customer specific IDP
    A hosting provider may setup Landscape Identity Providers (these Identity Providers will be shown in the IDP drop down list when adding an IDP user for customers across the landscape) in addition to customer specific Identity Providers (these Identity Providers will be shown together with the landscape IDPs in the IDP drop down list when adding an IDP user under a specific customer).

Documentation

For further reading, be sure to check out "Identity and Authentication Management in SAP Business One Cloud" How-to-Guide, walking you through implementation, usage and behavioral changes in SAP Business One Cloud when working with IAM.

I hope this Blog was useful to you as an introduction to the Identity and Authentication Management solution in SAP Business One Cloud PL 20. I look forward to hearing about your experience from working with IAM in SAP Business One Cloud, be sure to leave your feedback in the comments section below.

entry.title: Re: EC Time Off Leave Report (via Canvas Report)

entry.title: Approve purchase order - SAP Fiori App
entry.content:
Hello experts, a pleasure to talk to you.
Today I have the following query:
I need to implement Fiori : Approve purchase order application (App ID: F0402A) on my SAP S/4 HANA Cloud Private system. (https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F0402A')/S32)
I've searched a lot but I'm just meager with the information I got, so I would like to know if someone could help me to find my way to implement this application.
My doubts are:
1) What is My inbox? and how does it relate to Approve purchase order.
2) Is there any demo video to see how the application works?
3) I only have to install the Front-End Components and the Back-end Components, and the application will start working?
4) Can I only implement the Approve purchase order application or should I implement some other application together with this one for its correct operation?

Of course I thank anyone who can help me, because it is a big problem I need to solve. Thank you very much in advance to everyone!

Regards, Max.

entry.title: Re: Dump on HR_EVENT_UPDATE - DBSQL Duplicate key
entry.content:
Could you post your code in characters (menu ... then </>)

How do you currently handle duplicates

  • Duplicate records received (for example, when an employee swipes his badge twice in less than 1 min, do you check on the badge application or before calling the BAPI)
  • Received record is alreay in infotype 2011 / TEVEN

entry.title: Re: Payment Term configuration not obtaining the desired...
entry.content: I have already tried doing this like you say in tx. OBB8, you may try to configure a term with settings in the "Payment Terms" section as Percentage: 0,000 No. of Days: 30 Fixed Day: 10 Additional months: 0 Note that under "Baseline date for calculation", "Fixed Date" and "Additional Months" should be left as '0' and the "Default for baseline date" should be the Document Date. But this is not working because I put the Document date 18.05.2024 and the calculated Net due date is being 10.06.2024 instead od 10.07.2024 which is what I would expect

entry.title: Production kit
entry.content:
Dear SAP colleagues, 

I would like to reach a question which I have not been able to resolve in SAP. We have a machine in the plant which can produce let's say with two different molds: 

  • Mold A would produce:
    1. Material A = 1 PC
    2. Material B = 1 PC
    3. Material D = 1 PC
  • Mold B:
    1. Material A = 1 PC
    2. Material B = 1 PC
    3. Material C = 1 PC

I try to deliver a solution in which the MRP is able to pick the corresponding production version optimizing the stocks depending on the requirements on C or D.

Could you please help me with any option?

Thanks and regards!

entry.title: SAP RAP Dynamic Feature Control Trigger after Determination - Managed Draft App
entry.content:
Hi,

I've a managed draft-enabled RAP app. I've setup a determination on modify that updates a few fields of the child entity when 1 field of the parent entity is updated. I've also setup side-effects through BAS annotations(since I'm on OP 2022) to have the changes reflected immediately in draft. I've setup dynamic feature control on these target fields of the child entity such that they should be displayed read-only when the source field of the parent entity has a value and unrestricted otherwise. The problem is that the feature control does not work as soon as I change the source field. I've to refresh the page/reload the instance for the feature control to take effect. How can I setup the app to have the fields of the child entity rendered read-only immediately after the determination updates these fields? In short, I want the feature control to trigger for the child entity immediately after the determination of the parent entity.

Regards,

Saurabh

entry.title: Re: RAP Action: The command "COMMIT-ENTITIES" is not allo...
entry.content:
See next extract from SAP help "COMMIT ENTITIES must be explicitly specified for change operations
that are triggered outside of a behavior implementation".

That means it's not required to call COMMIT ENTITIES inside an action.

entry.title: Re: Hi team please let me know the steps to perform stock reconciliation with 3pl and ewm
entry.content: I wasn't referring to inventory reconciliation specifically... I was commenting based on the integration of a 3PL with EWM.

entry.title: Re: Production kit
entry.content:
Hello @cvivo_pelzer 

I don't think you can implement such a requirement in standard. You'd need to put version selection logic into the MD_MODIFY_PRODVERS BAdI enhancement.

Once I faced a similar requirement - a customer wanted manual production version selection in production order based on components availability. I had to develop a custom search help for the production version field. The search looped through all production versions, exploded their BOMs, checked availability of components and presented the results in a nice ALV tree list. Then a user could select a version, which components were available.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: Modify Z* Db table not working
entry.content: What exactly is the bug? Do i mandatorily need to use Alpha Conv for the INVNO?

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud Live data connection to SAP SuccessFactors
entry.content:
@Maheshwar_Singh 

This is great ! and very nicely articulated - easy to understand . One Question , I understand post the Live Connection , we can directly start creating Stories the same way we create today in Embedded SuccessFactors Story Reports - as you have mentioned , it will be amazing as it will be no brainer from some one who has worked on SuccessFactors Story Reports to start leveraging SAC to create Reports - consuming data from different Shemas

The Question I have is , if there will be option to consume the already existing Story Reports - like I have 50 Story Reports pre built - can we consume them ? 

 

Thanks

Aylwin

entry.title: Re: Click to expand subreport
entry.content:
Hi carmine1, you can do this via a shared var.

In the main report create a new formula with syntax like:

whileprintingrecords;
shared numbervar imInTheMainReport := 1

and put this in the section of the subreport you're trying to control.

Now in the subreport, go to the sections that you want to leave suppressed when you're in the main report and put in a conditional suppression formula like the following.

whileprintingrecords;
shared numbervar imInTheMainReport = 1

Now when you open the subreport you'll see all of the sections.

entry.title: Introducing Business Builders: Free SAP gamified learning >> with SAP Analytics Cloud!
entry.content:
We are thrilled to introduce Business Builders, a gaming platform developed by HEC Montréal in close collaboration with us SAP🎮 for educators around the world.

Business Builders offers gamified learning experiences that equip students with crucial data storytelling and decision-making skills needed for success in the modern business landscape while developing in-demand SAP Analytics Cloud skills.

With initial scenarios in key areas such as sustainable business practices, supply chain resilience, and international business expansion, Business Builders offers a comprehensive learning experience  to help students bring out their best in the competitive global job market. 🌍

And the best: This new educational offering is free to all educators and their students globally.

𝐑𝐞𝐚𝐝𝐲 𝐭𝐨 𝐛𝐫𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐭𝐡𝐢𝐬 𝐢𝐧𝐧𝐨𝐯𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐯𝐞 𝐥𝐞𝐚𝐫𝐧𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐭𝐨𝐨𝐥 𝐭𝐨 𝐲𝐨𝐮𝐫 𝐜𝐥𝐚𝐬𝐬𝐫𝐨𝐨𝐦? 

Professors, lecturers and teachers can apply to register for a Business Builders account for free ➡ www.businessbuilders.games. Preregister for training starting June 25. Let’s make learning fun ✨ and impactful together! 

👍 Give us a kudos if you like these news! 👍

Your SAP University Alliances Team

entry.title: Re: Modify Z* Db table not working
entry.content: Only you can see and compare the values you have in the internal table and in the database table (any difficulty?) Now if it's about the very basic concept of internal and external values, then apply the conversion exit to convert external values into internal values.

entry.title: Re: Connection string Crystal reports in SAP B1 HANA vers...
entry.content:
Hi Jerusha, are you still having this issue? If so what's the Command syntax?

Here's a suggestion that might work to grab the proper connection string:

  1. Create a new report connecting directly to the Calc View (not a Command)
  2. Go to the Database menu and then Show SQL Query > copy that syntax
  3. Create a new report using a Command, with the copied syntax...what is the result?

entry.title: Re: Chatp GPT, help me refactor my class
entry.content:
Love the article!

Here, you are the "coach" and GPT is just a pupil. Now imagine, that you don't know the code and the problem inside-out, you barely understand what does it do and ChatGPT makes the refactoring. Will you catch all the crucial mistakes it made? Syntax check will catch the syntax, pretty printer will fix formatting, other tools will further improve the code, but if ChatGPT has mutated the logic, it will probably be more time-consuming to fix it, than doing the refactoring by yourself.

On the other hand, genAI can do the simple things:

  • generate some test classes
  • help you with reg-ex
  • help with CDS annotations

On the other hand it's better to write TDD and regex should be avoided as possible, as they are equally difficult to read as to write :).

entry.title: Re: How can I configure SSO with Kerberos in the old way...
entry.content:
Please look into SAP notes:

  • 2440692 - Central Note for SNC Client Encryption 2.0
  • 2564084 - SNC Kerberos Configuration for SAP GUI troubleshooting - Guided Answers

Also, there are some YOUTUBE videos that show all the steps needed: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=BlqZxgfzW6s

entry.title: Re: Configure Your Business Processes: An Introduction to SAP Central Business Configuration
entry.content:
Hello Andreas,

This password is generated during the initial setup of IAS, IPS, and SAP Central Business Configuration in the context of trust configuration. It's generally not advised to change it.

However, it can be retrieved from the backend by the responsible Cloud Operations team. For that, you would have to raise a ticket under component X4-CBC-SRV. Hope that helps.

Best regards, 
Fabian

entry.title: Re: SAP TCODES USAGE BY USERS
entry.content: Official function modules SWNC_*. was a good suggestion. My developer colleague will look into it.

entry.title: Re: SAP TCODES USAGE BY USERS
entry.content: i like this approach. But what to input if we want to know users who use only me2n like in your example

entry.title: What is the Difference between RISE with SAP and Grow with SAP Offering ?
entry.content:
Hello 

Can someone please explain fundamental difference between RISE with SAP and Grow with SAP offering from SAP ? I have read many blogs however all fail to explain the fundamental difference in terms of licensing, service delivery method, Solutions included and platform supported .

Appreciate explanation in this regard .

-Thanks 

Mamta Chauhan

entry.title: How to add Consolidation FS Item by Time Version to the Group Data Analysis app?
entry.content:
Hi experts,

This is my first time working with an FS Item configuration, so I might be missing some detail, however, I'll try to explain the issue as much as possible.

I have a requirement for extending the Group Data Analysis app (W0135) on a SAP S/4HANA 2022 on-premises system. The users need to be able to add some of the information available in the customising table "Consolidation FS Item by Time Version" (fincs_fsiassgmt).

So far, I have gone with the ABAP Extensibility route and added a custom CDS extension to the GDA query (C_CnsldtnRpt30Q) and the underlying Matrix Consolidation views (I_MatrixCnsldtnFndnCube and I_MatrixCnsldtnFndnPeriodic). With this, the fields become available in the Query. However, there's an unexpected result when running the query.

The FS Item has two validity periods in the customising table (using CDS view I_CnsldtnFSItemByTimeVersion), one on FYP range 0000.000 to 2023.004 and the other on FYP 2023.005 to 9999.999

 

But the resulting query run with YTD Period Mode shows the Elimination Selection field as unchanged only until FYP > 2024.000. 

The users are expecting to see the YTD mode to reflect the configuration on the FS Item Attribute, even if there is no data in ACDOCU until FYP 2024.000.

Here's an extract of my implementation of the link between the CDS views:

extend view C_CnsldtnRpt30Q with YGL_COBL_E_GRPDTAPPEXT
{
// ...
  @AnalyticsDetails.query.axis: #FREE
  @AnalyticsDetails.query.display: #KEY_TEXT
  MatrixCnsldtnFndnCube.CnsldtnFSItemElimination,

  @AnalyticsDetails.query.axis: #FREE
  @AnalyticsDetails.query.display: #KEY_TEXT
  MatrixCnsldtnFndnCube.CnsldtnFSItemCustomerSpecific
}

 

extend view I_MatrixCnsldtnFndnCube with YGL_COBL_E_MTRXCNSLDFNCEXT
{
// ...
  association [0..1] to I_CnsldtnFSItemByTimeVersion as _CsFSItemAttribute on  $projection.consolidationchartofaccounts =  _CsFSItemAttribute.ConsolidationChartOfAccounts
                                                                           and $projection.financialstatementitem       =  _CsFSItemAttribute.CnsldtnFinancialStatementItem
                                                                           and $projection.consolidationversion         =  _CsFSItemAttribute.ConsolidationVersion
                                                                           and $projection.fiscalyearperiod             >= _CsFSItemAttribute.FromFiscalYearPeriod
                                                                           and $projection.fiscalyearperiod             <= _CsFSItemAttribute.ToFiscalYearPeriod

// ...
  @ObjectModel.foreignKey.association: '_CnsldtnFSItemElimination'
  _CsFSItemAttribute.CnsldtnFSItemElimination,

  @ObjectModel.foreignKey.association: '_CnsldtnFSItemCustSpcfc'
  _CsFSItemAttribute.CnsldtnFSItemCustomerSpecific,

  _CsFSItemAttribute,
  _CsFSItemAttribute._CnsldtnFSItemElimination,
  _CsFSItemAttribute._CnsldtnFSItemCustSpcfc,

}
extend view I_MatrixCnsldtnFndnPeriodic with YGL_COBL_E_IMCFNDNPEXT
{
//...
  association [0..1] to I_CnsldtnFSItemByTimeVersion as _CsFSItemAttribute on  $projection.consolidationchartofaccounts =  _CsFSItemAttribute.ConsolidationChartOfAccounts
                                                                           and $projection.financialstatementitem       =  _CsFSItemAttribute.CnsldtnFinancialStatementItem
                                                                           and $projection.consolidationversion         =  _CsFSItemAttribute.ConsolidationVersion
                                                                           and $projection.fiscalyearperiod             >= _CsFSItemAttribute.FromFiscalYearPeriod
                                                                           and $projection.fiscalyearperiod             <= _CsFSItemAttribute.ToFiscalYearPeriod

// ...

  @ObjectModel.foreignKey.association: '_CnsldtnFSItemElimination'
  _CsFSItemAttribute.CnsldtnFSItemElimination,

  @ObjectModel.foreignKey.association: '_CnsldtnFSItemCustSpcfc'
  _CsFSItemAttribute.CnsldtnFSItemCustomerSpecific,
  _CsFSItemAttribute,
  _CsFSItemAttribute._CnsldtnFSItemElimination,
  _CsFSItemAttribute._CnsldtnFSItemCustSpcfc,

//...
}

 

  1. Am I missing some functionality of the FS Item configuration? Or is it an error in the logic of the CDS views that retrieve the data from the Consolidation tables?
  2. Are CDS views too complicated for this task? Is there any other way to implement this? Maybe using the Custom Fields and Logic app?

In any case, many thanks in advance for any help or guidance that you can provide.

entry.title: User/Approver did not receive email notification for job requisition approval
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

Greetings for the day!

I have below issue with one of my clients. 

One of the User did not receive email notification for job requisition approval.

User was on the approval chain for one of the Job Requisition Created and did not receive an email to approve the requisition.

I have enabled Admin Center > E-Mail Notification Templates > Document Routing Notification and Specified the Different Template for Each Form under Job Requisition. 

Verified the Job Requisition Route map who are involved the approval chain and also the Manage Recruiting groups. 

Please let me know any configuration is missing in the system in order to receive the email notifications.

Thanks in advance.

Pradeep

SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting

entry.title: Re: Single item plan for two accounting principles (IFRS...
entry.content:
Hi @tiagoaraujo , Group Reporting operates with Financial Statement Items which are mapped to GL accounts in S/4HANA Finance. You can have multiple FI chart of accounts mapped to group chart of accounts in GR for different companies.

Please see GR help on FSI integration with GL accounts: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/4ebf1502064b406c964b0911adfb3f01/68c83a5cfb1c47589752769a2878b91a.html?version=2023.001

entry.title: Re: Why SAP killed Appgyver and made everything worse?
entry.content:
Hi , can anyone help me?

I'm trying to find the way to delete firebase storage file? easy to upload but no flow function to delete? 

I wonder if i would find it on old Appgyver forum ... there was answer for every struggle or question , it was visible and easy to navigate through. 

Is there any chance we could have a download link to taken off content ,please??

entry.title: Re: SRM_VE_GROUPING / SRM_VE_WEIGHTING with BW4HANA
entry.content: Check the authorizations for a particular user id. Or SE43 for further investigation.

entry.title: Re: Create a custom MDF Object with multiple records
entry.content:
Hi - I have a requirement to have multi-attachment fields with the same effective date. 

I have created two custom MDF objects named as "cust_attachment" and "cust_ addattachment". The parent custom MDF acts as a holder for the multiple child record, which is the second custom MDF.

 I have configured the Associations on the Parent MDF, with One to Many multiplicity and my second MDF Object as the Destination.

For some reason, when i go to UI and don't see an option to add multiattachment field with the same effective date?

Any suggestion on how this can be accomplished?

entry.title: Re: Integrate Standalone SAP HANA Cloud DB to SAP BW4HANA
entry.content:
Connecting a Standalone SAP HANA Cloud DB to SAP BW4HANA requires careful consideration, especially given your high volume requirements.

ABAP Utility for Data Extraction (ZCL_HANA_CATALOG_UTILITIES):

Pros: This utility provides a straightforward way to extract data from SAP HANA tables directly into BW4HANA.
Cons: It may not be the most efficient method for handling large volumes of data, especially if you're dealing with millions of records.
Challenges: Performance issues may arise due to the sheer volume of data, potentially leading to longer extraction times and resource consumption.

ODP – Information View Connection Type:

Pros: Offers a standardized method for data extraction that leverages SAP's Operational Data Provisioning (ODP) framework.
Cons: Requires setting up and maintaining Information Views, which adds complexity to the solution.
Challenges: While ODP is designed for high-volume data extraction, performance tuning may be necessary to ensure optimal throughput, especially with large datasets.

Pseudo Delta Pushdown:

Pros: Provides a mechanism for efficiently extracting delta changes from the source system, reducing the amount of data transferred.
Cons: Implementation complexity can be higher compared to full data extraction methods.
Challenges: Ensuring that pseudo delta mechanisms are properly configured and maintained is crucial for accurate and timely data replication.

Ultimately, the best approach will depend on factors such as your specific data model, existing infrastructure, and resource constraints.

entry.title: Clean Core related enhancements in the Solution Standardization Board for RISE with SAP
entry.content:
This blog builds on the previous post I made earlier in January about two types of governance in the transformation project that SAP Activate provides. You can find the blog post on the SAP Activate community page - Two Key Types of Governance Bodies for your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Project Success. In that post, I discussed the traditional governance setup for managing the escalation paths in typical projects for scope, risk, timeline/schedule, etc., and specific solution governance for driving the proper design decisions for the business solution itself. This type of governance is critical in the deployment of RISE with SAP solution as it directly ties to the clean core strategy-based deployment.

Clean core strategy has become a synonym for building the solutions the right way to ensure agility, long-term maintainability and ability to change. Over the past months, SAP experts have worked diligently to incorporate several significant enhancements into SAP Activate that help customers embrace the clean core strategy. These improvements further enhance the Solution Standardization Board governance and bring additional focus on proper planning for clean core, regular inspections of the adherence to clean core strategy as well as improved on-boarding experience. These enhancements have been launched in the past few weeks under the umbrella of RISE with SAP Methodology that uses SAP Activate at its core while enhancing the experience with a tightly integrated toolchain including SAP Signavio, LeanIX, and SAP Cloud ALM.

 

 

 

In today's article, we will specifically dive into the enhancements to the Solution Standardization Board and in future posts we will look at other aspects of the clean core strategy and how it is embedded into SAP Activate guidance. The recent releases of SAP Activate for RISE with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition has introduced following updates that are critical to embrace for customers deploying their RISE with SAP solution. Let's take a look at each.

Enhanced Five Golden Rules

One of the key enhancements delivered in Q1 of 2024 is the enhanced five golden rules that fully align with the five dimensions of clean core - process, data, extensibility, integration, and operational excellence. While the previous iteration of Golden Rules covered most aspects, it didn't specifically call out the importance of data (though it was implied as part of the Golden Rules for embracing standard business processes). In the latest iteration, the golden rules have evolved, and the data dimension has also been included. You can see the progress of the guidance over the year below in the image, which shows the evolution from pure process-focused guidance to comprehensive coverage for all aspects of the proper solution design.

 

You can see the latest version of the 5 Golden Rules aligned to clean core dimensions below:

 

1. Start with pre-configured processes, foster cloud mindset by adhering to Fit-to-Standard and Agile deployment detailed in SAP Activate

  • Adopt SAP standard processes SAP Best Practices, SAP Enterprise Management Layer (*)(**), SAP Standard Content  or Partner templates(**) for fit-to-standard
  • Where the pre-configured processes do not exist - process configuration and personalization should follow SAP-recommended configuration standards (IMG)
  • Utilize SAP Fiori UX and pre-defined processes and data

2. Build your solution on quality data (configuration, master, and transactional) to leverage innovations

  • Ensure data quality including accuracy, completeness, consistency, timeliness, validity, and uniqueness.
  • Manage data volumes to optimize resources utilization and ensure data privacy.

3. Use modern integration technologies

  • Follow SAP Integration Solution Advisory Methodology.
  • Use SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) for Integration.
  • Leverage SAP Business Accelerator Hub for available APIs.

4. Use modern extensions technologies

  • Follow guidance from Extend SAP S/4HANA in the cloud and on premise with ABAP based extensions –
    (A) Extend solution using released remote or local SAP APIs; (B) Extend SAP objects only via predefined extension points; and (C) Leverage cloud-enabled and released technologies.
  • Use SAP Application Extension Methodology to develop modern extensions
  • Retire custom ABAP extensions that you no longer need and renovate existing custom ABAP extension using cloud extension techniques.

5. Ensure transparency on deviations from these Golden Rules for Operational Excellence

  • Any deviation has to be clearly documented as part of the deployment process; this will help the customer replace these with standard capabilities if they are offered in the future.
  • Use standard capabilities of SAP Application Lifecycle Management tools to document the solution.

These enhancements have been in SAP Activate since late Q1 of this year. They are also accompanied by updates to the Solution Standardization Board template, which provides more details about the structure of the governing body, processes to follow, cadence, and participation from all parties involved in the transformation program.

Now we will look at some of the more recent enhancements that we added to SAP Activate this week.

SSB Deviation Tracker and Key Decision Document

While the Solution Standardization Board in SAP Activate provides a framework for tracking and documenting the deviation from the Five Golden Rules it was recently enhanced with two new templates to support customers in tracking the deviations and for documenting the key design decisions during the project. The two additional templates as assigned to the Solution Standardization Board task and below is a short description of the two assets:

  • SSB Deviation Tracker Template - This template is used to keep track of the design decisions according to the 5 Golden Rules. It is a running list of extensions, integrations, data design decisions, and process design decisions that have been prepared for SSB and reviewed by the governance body. It is the embodiment of the 5th golden rule to document the deviations. Note that the tracker can be also considered the running list of technical debt that should be regularly reviewed for potential "back to standard" items after the go-live. Especially after an upgrade or when new capabilities are delivered into the RISE with SAP environment. 
  • Key Decision Document - for each entry in the deviation tracker the requestor of the deviation should prepare materials for the SSB to make a decision. This template is designed to help with documenting the required decision, outlining the alternatives that were considered, and recommendations for proceeding. The build and test work on the deviation only can start after approval by the SSB. 

We recommend all customers deploying RISE with SAP solutions establish a strong governance body for adherence to the clean core strategy that is reflected in the 5 Golden Rules. It doesn't matter if you call it a Solution Standardization Board, Design Review Board, or another name. The key is that this body works on steering the design decisions to both enable your organization to benefit from your RISE with SAP investment and at the same time help manage the tendency to overdevelop custom code even for requirements that are not differentiating. 

In the next installment, we will take a look at the new clean core success plan, clean core quality gates, and onboarding activities.

entry.title: Re: Messages in SAC Handling with BW Live Connection
entry.content:
you could write a custom script that listens for the appearance of validation messages and stores them in a custom component or data structure within your SAC story.

or

Depending on your setup, you might be able to configure logging in BW/BPC to capture these validation messages. This would involve configuring your BW/BPC system to log the messages generated during query execution, and then accessing these logs separately from SAC.

entry.title: Re: How to share IQ multiplex DBFiles opened with (O_DIRE...
entry.content:
This seems to be basically the same question that you asked previously here: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/how-can-we-open-iq-16-1-under-suse-15-with-o-direct-flag/qaq-p/13706710

 

SAP IQ always uses direct I/O and nothing needs to be done for that to happen. 

Is there a particular issue or error that you are running into?

Are you preparing for an OS upgrade?

Are you replacing/upgrading you storage hardware?

entry.title: Re: BW extractor 2LIS_02_SCL showing different results fo...
entry.content:
REEWR (Cumulative Receipt Value):

This field usually reflects cumulative values for receipt quantities or values in purchasing.
It is typically sourced from tables such as EKET (Scheduling Agreement Schedule Lines) for scheduling agreement data.
The cumulative values can also be derived from the aggregation of data from the MSEG (Document Segment: Material) table which records material movements.

DMBTR (Amount in Local Currency):

This field indicates the amount in the local currency.
The primary table for this data is the EKPO (Purchasing Document Item) table, where the local currency amounts for purchase order items are maintained.
For goods movements, DMBTR values are recorded in the BSEG (Accounting Document Segment) table, which stores the financial document data.


REEWR (Cumulative Receipt Value): This field is usually accumulated from the values of goods receipts (GRs) and invoices received (IRs). It is supposed to reflect the total value received against a purchase order or schedule line.

DMBTR (Amount in Local Currency): This field reflects the value of transactions in the local currency, and it is directly sourced from financial postings and invoice entries.

Analyzing the Specific Case:

Initial GR and IR: When the goods receipt is done, the system updates the REEWR and DMBTR with the received quantity and value.
Incorrect IR and Reversal: If an invoice is posted at an incorrect price, it affects REEWR. When this invoice is reversed and a new one is posted, ideally the system should correct REEWR to reflect the accurate cumulative value.
Discrepancy in Calculation: If there’s a discrepancy in REEWR and DMBTR values due to incorrect IR postings and reversals, the calculated BWGEO will be incorrect.
Ensuring Correct Calculation
To ensure that the calculation is based on the DMBTR value, you can take the following steps:

Investigate Table Data:

EKET: Check the quantities and values in the schedule lines.
EKBE: Analyze the history of goods movements and invoice receipts. Look for any anomalies or incorrect postings.
BSEG: Verify the accounting entries to ensure they reflect the correct amounts.
Adjust Extractor Logic:

Custom Logic for BWGEO: You may need to adjust the logic in your extractor to prioritize DMBTR over REEWR for calculating BWGEO.
Formula Adjustment: Ensure the calculation formula explicitly uses DMBTR for deriving the invoice price.
ABAP
Copy code
BWGEO = EKET-MENGE * EKBE-DMBTR / EKBE-MENGE
Data Consistency Check:

Ensure that the data in ECC (ERP Central Component) tables (like EKET, EKBE, and BSEG) is consistent.
Implement consistency checks in your data extraction routines to flag discrepancies.
Technical Implementation
Enhancement Spot in Extractor: Implement an enhancement in the 2LIS_02_SCL extractor to ensure it uses DMBTR for the BWGEO calculation.

ABAP
Copy code
IF <some_condition>. " Check if the scenario matches the problematic case
BWGEO = EKET-MENGE * DMBTR / EKBE-MENGE.
ELSE.
BWGEO = REEWR / EKET-MENGE. " Default calculation
ENDIF.
ABAP Debugging: Use ABAP debugging tools to step through the extractor logic and ensure that it’s correctly fetching and calculating the values.

Please focus on calculations.

entry.title: Re: standard LIKP table not getting activated after append structure and doing se14
entry.content: There are around 25000 records in the SAP Data Base table LIKP. Its more than 2 hours and the background job is still in Active mode. I am loosing hope now. I even doubt if this job would be Finished by morning. What should I have to do if the job is in Active even after 12 hours ?

entry.title: Re: What is the difference between these two offerings fr...
entry.content:
Thanks for the response. However, the confusion around the requirement for EC implementation came from the below issue - 

I tried the Learning Journey - Configuring SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Core.  In the Setting Up the Training System section,  under the setting up the training environment, this paragraph provides an instruction to download a standard configuration files - "Your practice environment for the THR81 course is already configured for use. However, original customer instances require preliminary provisioning setup and configuration. To learn more about the initial configuration tasks, such as Provisioning settings need to be activated, review the implementation guide on the SAP Help Portal. To see the standard Employee Central Configuration files provided for a customer implementation, visit the Download Center"

When I clicked through this download center link (or go directly to the download center and follow instructions in the note 3153500 - How to download config files), I get the message No data found.  I opened a ticket to support but the response from support was quite interesting. Per support, my S-user Id did not have an SF EC instance assigned (we do not have EC implemented yet), so the config files are not available for my S-user Id. 

On the one hand the training instruction in the learning journey says, go here and download the config files to learn more but on the other the access is being denied because there is no SF EC instance assigned.  This looks very weird.

entry.title: Re: Custom sorting with BW 7.3 source system at Webi 4.3
entry.content:
Create new webi report and new document and select BW query as datasource.

Insert the data for the objects Country, state, city, town and depth into the report.

Custom Sorting Logic in Webi:

Create the SortKey variable with the formula: [Depth] & [Country] & [State] & [City] & [Town].

Apply sorting using the SortKey variable to ensure the hierarchical data is displayed correctly.

entry.title: Re: SAC Model defined threshold with bw live query models...
entry.content:
For example, Create Restricted Measure for Sales:

Open your SAC story and navigate to the model.
Select Sales and create a restricted measure where Sales > 10000.
Name this measure Sales Above Threshold.

Conditional Formatting in Story:

Use the Sales Above Threshold measure in your visualizations.
Apply conditional formatting to highlight these values, for instance:
Red for values above 10000.
Green for values below or equal to 10000.
Combining SAC Features with Custom Scripting

Use SAC Scripting Capabilities:

SAC scripting (Analytics Designer) can offer more advanced customization.
Write scripts to evaluate thresholds dynamically if your use case allows for scripting.

Dynamic Input Using Input Controls:

Allow users to set threshold values dynamically using input controls (if working with planning models or if some workaround is feasible).
Use these input values in your custom calculations or scripts to dynamically adjust thresholds.

Static thresholds are easier to implement with live models.

For dynamic thresholds, consider workarounds like using input controls and scripting.

Note: Be mindful of performance implications, especially when working with large datasets and complex calculations in live models.

entry.title: Re: Setting up Payroll Processing with SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll (Point-to-point i
entry.content:
Hello @RubaElhafiz ,

Is it possible to use the same connection method (RFC) from the payroll system to S4HANA RISE?

entry.title: link blue boxing name to work order sap
entry.content:
for our technicians we need to do some blueboxing where materials are combined in a box.

al those boxes have a number for example C203 or B201.

we would like to put the C203 somewhere in the order but there is nothing we can find that is also printed on the work orders and that we can see in the list we track in iw37N.

is there a field that we can use for this?

thanks in advance

entry.title: SAP Community Voice: June 5, 2024, Edition
entry.content:
As announced in the last two editions of SAP Community Voice, and in this What's New post, we are phasing out our newsletter.

We'll send out one final edition, scheduled for June 19. After that, SAP Community Voice will be available exclusively here, in this What's New section of the SAP Community site. In fact, we've already started to add SAP Community Voice content to What's New (but, since you're reading this post, you probably already guessed that).

If you enjoy SAP Community Voice content, then please subscribe to What's New now. It's easy. Just go to the What's New landing page, click the "Options" menu in the upper right, and select Subscribe.

By subscribing to What's New (and setting your notification settings to your liking), you'll still be able to receive the community news, highlights, and other content you've come to expect from SAP Community Voice.

We'll be back in approximately two weeks with a new SAP Community Voice post. For now, enjoy this sampling of popular SAP Community content.

 

Featured Content

 

Changes: SAP Analytics Cloud Classic Stories and Analytic Applications 

This blog post explains the decision behind the deprecation of the classic experience for this product, the benefits of the optimized experience, and the key timelines for the transition. By reading, you can prepare for the change and make the most of the optimized experience! 

Read the blog post

 

Don't Miss Your Chance to Join Global Running Day (Through June 9) 

June 5 is Global Running Day. If you couldn't participate on the actual date (either virtually or in person with other members at Tan in Melbourne, Australia), we have good news for you! You still have until June 9 to join the fun. Not a runner? Walkers are welcome, too! 

Read the blog post

 

Blog Series: SAP Datasphere Multilingual Support - Translation Dashboard 

The second in a series on multilingual capabilities in SAP Datasphere, this post provides guidance on how to use the new dashboard for translating metadata in SAP Datasphere that SAP Analytics Cloud can leverage to display the stories or dashboards in several language. 

Read the blog post

 

Extracting Data Sources from SAP S/4HANA ODP to SAP Datasphere

A data source enabled for operational data provisioning (ODP) allows the transfer of data between systems efficiently, and this blog post explores how to extract data consistently and reliably from SAP S/4HANA (with an ODP-enabled data source) to SAP Datasphere. 

Read the blog post

 

Community News

 

News and Other Resources for Community Members 

With Q&A forums, discussion areas, and blog boards, SAP Community provides valuable information for members looking to learn more about SAP. But what if you want to learn more about SAP Community itself? Make sure to subscribe to What's New, read our help section, watch our how-to videos, and take the Tour the SAP Community 2024 tutorial. 

 

Register for Exciting and Educational Events in June 

SAP Sapphire may be wrapping up in Orlando, but SAP Sapphire Barcelona is coming up soon -- plus our events section is filled with SAP CodeJam, SAP Inside Track, and SAP Stammtisch meetups, as well as other online and in-person events, including Hands-on with the btp CLI and APIs and ABAP CodeRetreat OnSite in Bern.

 

Dress for Success at SAP Community Events 

Speaking of SAP Community events (how's that for a segue?), have you signed up for an in-person session and you're not sure what to wear? Why not make a fashion statement with an item from our online store? There you'll find shirts, hoodies, and pins branded with SAP Community -- everything you need to ensure you're the sharpest-dressed attendee!

entry.title: Re: How to show total at the end of SAC tables created by...
entry.content:
Refer: 

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/sap-analytics-cloud-sum-table-columns/qaq-p/12626580

entry.title: Re: Not possible to display SAC Live BW Query Texts in Ge...
entry.content:
You can display it either in German or English.


-In SAC  go to Settings and Language section.
-In BW go to SE63 and Enter the query technical name and maintain translations for the desired language (eg. German and English). and check the text elements are updated in RSRT.

entry.title: Re: SAP CPI and S4HANA, Payment Advice Notes via EDI
entry.content:
Hi @mk_eventmesh , 

please clarify first, is this request for S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition or a different S/4HANA product? You'd be able to find the appropriate integration scenario(s) on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub at https://api.sap.com. Select the right S/4HANA product for your purpose, then simply enter advice or payment and see which scenario best fulfills the business requirement. For example: Overview | SAP Enterprise Service Management Integration with SAP S/4HANA Cloud | SAP Business Accelerator Hub.

entry.title: Re: SAP FICO Periodic Posting ASKB posted higher Amount
entry.content: I would check the available reporting to see if you can find a report that can give you a break down per asset https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ERP/e054164e736046be90f4aec602391321/3070d8530439414de10000000a174cb4.html?locale=en-US

entry.title: DataSphere "Hierarchy with Directory" configuration problem
entry.content:
Hello,

I'm totally new to the SAP World, over 25 years MS-BI. But now starting with DS.

While trying to configure a Hierarchy with Directory I get everythign setup (at least it reports no errors) but when I try to create a model the hierarchy is totally ignored. I'l try to privode as much info as possible.

This is de model

Regio Dimension, Semantic is Dimension

Regio Hierarchy, Semantic is Hierary with Directory

Here I mssed a bit with the fieldnames but link is ok.

RegioHierarchyNames, Semantic Usage is Dimension

Depending on the samples I found on the web this should be it, but obviously I'm missing something ...

Any suggestions ?

entry.title: Re: Dear PM Gurus,Can anyone suggest me what is the shutd...
entry.content:
Hi @Sandeep7 ,

answers to questions like this you'll find in the SAP Learning content for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, here: Preparing and Scheduling Maintenance (sap.com)

A more specific answer might be, you can use different buckets for planning maintenance on assets. The learning content says, e.g. 'once-off' planning buckets (personally, I'd call them 'one-off', to distinguish from buckets for recurring maintenance) might, I quote, "help you to organize a planned shutdown or overhaul of an entire plant section, for example. When the maintenance planning buckets have been created and maintenance orders have been associated with particular planning buckets, you can manage your maintenance backlog. From a particular planning bucket, you navigate to the list of all the maintenance orders associated with this planning bucket." So, I suppose it less a type of maintenance order, more an association with a respective shutdown (one-off) planning bucket, right?

entry.title: Re: I have a requirement to run the iflow on 2 specific d...
entry.content:
Hi @km4 ,

I suppose your iFlow would need to have a timer flow step, looking like an analog clock face in the diagram. If it does (or if you can add this step to your iFlow ), tab 'Scheduler' should allow you to determine the start day(s) and time(s). See (e.g.) this blog for further insights: SAP Integration Suite – Advanced Scheduler Configu... - SAP Community

entry.title: I want to send only today's files to sftp using sftp sender adapter which polls every 10mins
Re: SAP Analytics Cloud Live data connection to SAP SuccessFactors
entry.content:
Great stuff.

Is there a release date for the SAP SuccessFactors Learning connection ?

entry.title: Re: Create a Dynamic Recipient Report based on Personalis...
entry.content:
Can do it in Business objects Design studio:

Create the two pages as described. Page 1 will show the branch name and branch manager, and Page 2 will display branch-specific sales figures.
Bind your data sources to the respective components (charts, crosstabs, etc.).

Use scripting within Design Studio to control the visibility of data. You can employ the USER() function to retrieve the current user's information and dynamically filter data based on this.

This script would be attached to the data source or component on Page 2 to ensure that branch managers only see their branch's data.

code:

var currentUser = USER();
DS_1.setFilter("BRANCH", "BRANCH_NAME = '" + currentUser + "'");
Note: Adjust the filtering logic to match your data model and user attribute configuration. Ensure the field used in the setFilter method matches the one in your data source that identifies the branch.

entry.title: Re: I want to send only today's files to sftp using sftp sender adapter which polls every 10mins
entry.content: If you only want to send files for today, then why not poll once a day instead of every 10 minutes?

entry.title: Re: I want to send only today's files to sftp using sftp...
entry.content: Hi, its polling and continuously sending all previous files as they are not letting us delete/archive them, My main concern is to only send file which has today's timestamp without modifying it. How can I achieve it

entry.title: Re: Step by Step instruction to Setup / Update IAS User Passwords in Mass using Postman
entry.content:
Thanks Yash!

How to add password as variable if you can advise please.

entry.title: Re: I want to send only today's files to sftp using sftp sender adapter which polls every 10mins
entry.content: You would need a regular expression based on the file naming format that would include the current date, so you would need to handle it with the combination of poll enrich and a start timer.

entry.title: Purchasing unit not being respected when converting Purchase Requisitions from PPDS
entry.content:
Our company uses alternate units of measure for purchasing that relate to the package type (drum, tote, etc.). These are all maintained in the purchasing tab of the material master, and in the PIR as the purchasing units that should be reflected on the Purchase Orders that we send internally as Stock Transports. When converting Purchase Requisitions manually in ME21N, these purchasing units work just fine, however, if purchase requisitions are converted via PPDS (product view), the quantity of the purchase order is always reflected as the base unit of measure.

Any ideas on how to fix this?

For context, we are on

entry.title: Lead Management: Create Lead only with contact and without "Existing Account" or "Company"
entry.content:
Dear Community,

We have a requirement to create Leads without an existing Account/Company, but it seems this is not possible.

While it's possible to create Opportunities using just a Contact, the Account is mandatory for Leads and cannot be unchecked in FineTuning / Leads / Involved Parties.

Additionally, there is no option in Scoping to create Leads without an existing Account/Company, unlike with Opportunities.

What can we do to address this requirement?

entry.title: Re: New or not to the SAP Community, share your story!
entry.content: Thanks for Information

entry.title: Request for copy of Production environment replacing current dev environment
entry.content:
We intend to copy the Current Production tenant : URL to replace current Dev environment URL

We intend to have all front end as well as backend copied along with all configurations.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Request for copy of Production environment replacing...
entry.content:
Hello,

SAP Cloud for Customer offers the option to create new tenants copying all or certain data from existing tenants.

As there are some constraints in the process, we suggest you to check on KBA 2136036 - How to Request a New Tenant for SAP Business ByDesign or SAP Cloud for Customer which documents the scenario in more details.

Kind regards,
Leonardo
SAP Support
Community:
SAP Business ByDesign: https://www.sap.com/community/topics/business-bydesign.html
SAP Cloud for Customer: https://www.sap.com/community/topics/cloud-for-customer.html

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori adoption enabled by Security Role Design
entry.content:
Thanks for the blog.

We are moving users slowly from SAP GUI to Fiori, but they will still have access to SAP GUI login for a while. 

In this case, should we keep granular authorizations both in backend roles and new Fiori roles? or do the authorizations embedded in Fiori roles apply to the SAP GUI backend as well?

What is the best-practice for moving users to Fiori:

1. Do we create brand new business roles that have Catalogs/Spaces as well as granular authorizations (ie. by company code, sales division, etc.), and just not use the existing GUI roles. Or:

2. Should we keep those auths in the backend roles and just have catalogs/spaces in the new business roles? 

3. Should we add the Fiori catalogs/spaces to existing backend roles?

Not clear what the right direction is.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: New to the SAP Community? A friend of someone already here?
entry.content: Thanks for this Info

entry.title: Re: Automating Purchase Order Creation from Purchase Requ...
entry.content:
Hi dear colleague,

I invite you to have a look on the documentation below, it may help you to cover your requirement. 

2647977 - Cloud Automatic Creation of Purchase Orders

Best Regards,

Julio

entry.title: Re: Submissions for "SAP HANA Cloud multi-model" developer challenge: Week 1 - Setup
entry.content:
My week1 submission here.

In Step8, I first executed the command as-is (`sh ~/sap/hdbclient/hdbsql -U myDBAdmin`), and got an error:

After reviewing the screenshot below the instructions, I realized that 'sh' was not required.

entry.title: Re: odata Basic authorization Sample java source code?
entry.content:
Update: it works for http Basic authorization only. 

My issue is actually about Https certification.

entry.title: Re: OData development & Registration on SAP BTP
entry.content:
Hi @CheriyanSam,

If my memory doesn't betray me, the service "OData Provisioning" was only available on Neo - which is being discontinued. And it was targeting customers that didn't have a SAP Gateway (FES) system running on-prem.

If you are moving to the cloud, it is most likely that you are going for an S/4HANA Private edition. In that case, the private edition already comes with the means for you to publish OData services without the need of a SAP Gateway system. It all runs on the same box. 

However, to comply with the ABAP Cloud guidelines (https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/abap-cloud/ba-p/13557273) you will have to rely on published APIs and CDS Views in order to "keep the core clean". 

This means that you will have two options:

1) Keep the developed OData services the way they are during the migration - and later you will have to figure out how to move them to the new ABAP Cloud guidelines. 

2) User ABAP Cloud guidelines to "repurpose" the OData services by adhering to the published APIs and exposed CDS Views. If non are available, use BTP to develop your OData service by consuming other standard APIs in your S/4HANA system. Adopt new ways of thinking about how you do your data integration with other systems, like using the Event-Driven Architecture - which allows you to capture ERP's events and send them to a queue for later processing in BTP. 

You can easily develop new OData services on BTP using CAP (Cloud Application Programming Model). This will allow you to leverage Java and NodeJS knowledge together with CDS syntax to easily define services based on local data (cloud database) or using Cloud SDK. This SDK allows you to easily consume any S/4HANA published API and it is fully integrated with BTP features like destinations.

Hope this helps.
Ivan

entry.title: Re: SAP Public Cloud Credit Management VKM4/VKM5 alternat...
entry.content:
Hi dear Colleague,

I recommend you to have a look on the documentation below, it will help you to find a better way to treat you needs.

Configuration for SAP Credit Management | SAP Help Portal
>> Approval Process for Documented Credit Decisions
>> Situation Handling in SAP Credit Management

Also in the customer master data its possible to see the list of a documents with credit block.

I hope it helps you on your journey for the better solution.

Regards,
Julio C.

entry.title: Re: Submissions for "SAP HANA Cloud multi-model" developer challenge: Week 1 - Setup
entry.content:

Have you had any other problems with commands or systems?

entry.title: Re: Returning table in classes
entry.content:
Hi!

I have a similar issue working with a method that class a function....I have already put the returning table as you said, but it didn't work 😪, could you help me? Please 

 method ZGET_DETALLE.
*    TYPES it_detalle TYPE ty_det.
     DATA: t_det TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF zccdetalle,
        l_detalle LIKE LINE  OF t_det .

    CALL FUNCTION 'ZF_DATOS_CLIENTE_LVAA'
      EXPORTING
        pncliente =  PNCLIENTE                " ELEMENTO DE LOS DATOS DEL CLIENTE
      IMPORTING
        pmensaje  = PMENSAJE                 " ELEMENTO DE DATO PARA MENSAJE*S
      TABLES
        ptcte     =  t_det             " ESTRUCTURA DETALLE CLIENTE
      .
    "PTDET[] = it_detalle[].
     IF sy-subrc = 0.
      ptdet[] = t_det[].
     ENDIF.

  CLEAR: l_detalle,t_det[].

  endmethod.

 In the interface

When I run the method appears this window

entry.title: MDK - how to get screen resolution?
entry.content:
How can I get screen resolution?

I need it for 'Module': 'extension-EditableDataTable'; calculate(set) the height of the component.
